text.title: heuristic-evaluation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for heuristic-evaluation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Evaluators collaborate during evaluation sessions — brainstorming together or reviewing findings as a group before individual assessments are complete.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Collaboration suppresses minority observations. Group discussion creates social pressure to conform; different evaluators' perspectives are the entire value of the method.

failure-mode.description: Using only one evaluator to save time or cost.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Single evaluator finds only ~35% of problems. Three evaluators find ~60%; the diminishing returns only appear beyond five. Single-evaluator assessments are unreliable.

failure-mode.description: Findings are too vague: "UI is confusing" or "buttons are unclear" without location or heuristic.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vague feedback is not actionable. Developers cannot implement changes without knowing which element, which heuristic, and what specific fix is recommended.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring severity ratings and implementing all findings equally.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: All issues are not equal. Severity 1 cosmetic issues do not deserve the same development effort as Severity 4 catastrophic issues. Resources must focus on high-impact problems first.

failure-mode.description: Running heuristic evaluation but never acting on findings.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Evaluation without implementation is waste. Without visible action on findings, evaluators and team learn that feedback is not valued and stop taking the process seriously.
